goal: Yrkesroll: NDT weld inspector / quality-control inspector (ONET SOC 51-9061, Inspectors/Testers/Sorters/Samplers/Weighers — non-destructive vision-inspection variant). A UR10 carries a wrist-mounted inspection Camera along a 200 mm pre-deposited weld seam at fixed standoff (0.08 m) and traversal speed (~0.02 m/s ≈ 10 s sweep). At each waypoint capture_camera_image grabs one frame; vision_analyze_scene asks a defect-class question per frame; add_vision_classifier_gate runs once over the seam-segment markers to produce a structured cube→class mapping (segment → 'ok' | 'porosity' | 'crack' | 'undercut'). get_world_transform on the wrist camera at each waypoint records the (x,y,z) sample so a coverage map (frames-per-cm-of-seam) can be derived offline. Success: ≥10 frames per cm of seam length AND a defect-class label produced for every frame. Pattern: vision NDT coverage scan — distinct from CP-NEW-welder-seam-track (creates the weld via impedance-compliant torch follow + arc proxy) and CP-NEW-yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack (discrete tack-welding gesture); this canonical performs NO welding, NO compliance contact, NO heat/arc physics — it only INSPECTS an already-formed seam to find defects post-weld. Realises backlog inspection-weld-seam-vision-ndt-001.
thoughts: Pattern: coverage scan over a 1-D seam path for vision-based DEFECT DETECTION. Not pick_place (no part moves), not sort (no destination routing — output is a defect report), not insert (no compliance contact), not navigate (fixed-base arm), not train (no SDG/RL loop). pattern_hint='other'. Discriminating structural_tags: isaac:industry.weld_ndt + isaac:inspection.vision_ndt + isaac:gesture.coverage_inspect + isaac:yrkesroll.weld_inspector.

DIFFERENTIATION from welding canonicals — THIS IS THE CRITICAL DESIGN AXIS. A28 (CP-NEW-welder-seam-track) CREATES the weld: impedance controller, torch contact, vision-corrected compliant follow, arc-proxy on a seam_target marker; structural_tags = isaac:assembly.weld_seam + isaac:compliance.impedance + isaac:motion.compliant_follow. A95 (CP-NEW-yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack) places discrete tack welds: arc duration, weave gesture, isaac:gesture.tack_weld. A115 INSPECTS an EXISTING weld — no compliance, no contact, no arc; the seam is already deposited and is rendered as a darker bead strip on top of a butt-joint pair. Tags MUST NOT collide: A115 does NOT use isaac:assembly.weld_seam (creation), DOES use isaac:industry.weld_ndt + isaac:inspection.vision_ndt (testing).

Robot: UR10 (backlog asset_dependencies=['UR10'] and motion_controller_preference=curobo). UR10 reach (~1.3 m) is comfortable for a 0.20 m seam over a workpiece centred at x≈0.55 m. Base on the ground at [0, 0, 0] with weld-joint at table height z≈0.84. Curobo handles the 6-DoF wrist orientation needed for a constant-look-at-seam pose.

Workpiece topology — TWO half-plates (Cube /World/PlateA + /World/PlateB) meeting along a butt joint at y=0 on a flat fixture; the deposited weld bead is a SEPARATE thin strip Cube /World/WeldSeam at the joint line, given a darker grey-brown material to mimic a fused bead and a semantic_label='weld_seam_bead'. The bead is 0.20 m × 0.008 m × 0.003 m. Three short sub-segments along the seam are tagged with semantic_label='seam_segment_*' for the classifier gate (segment_porosity at x=-0.07, segment_crack at x=0.00, segment_ok at x=+0.07 — three defect categories + clean reference, modelled by SEPARATE marker prims overlaying the bead). This lets add_vision_classifier_gate produce a per-segment classification structurally identical to A87 (inspector-reject) but routed to a DEFECT-CLASS dict instead of physical bins.

Wrist-mounted inspection camera — /World/UR10/tool0 has a child Camera at /World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera. configure_camera (deprecated alias) + set_camera_params sets focal_length=24 mm + horizontal_aperture=8.0 mm (close-range macro). set_camera_look_at orients the camera straight DOWN at the seam from a 0.08 m standoff (camera at tool0+offset[0,0,0.08], target at seam centre z=0.84). The camera-on-tool0 parenting means as the UR10 traces the seam, the camera viewpoint follows.

Seam traversal — 11 waypoints over a 0.20 m seam length, sample spacing 0.02 m. At v=0.02 m/s the sweep takes ~10 s; 11 samples / 20 cm = 5.5 samples/cm. To hit the ≥10-frame/cm success threshold the canonical doubles the sampling: BETWEEN every pair of waypoints, an INTERMEDIATE capture_camera_image is fired, yielding 21 captured frames over 20 cm = 10.5 frames/cm. waypoints have constant z=0.92 (seam top z=0.84 + standoff 0.08), constant orientation looking down. plan_trajectory plans the waypoint list as one curobo call; per-waypoint move_to_pose drives execution and capture_camera_image fires after each.

Vision pipeline — TWO complementary vision calls. (1) PER-WAYPOINT: vision_analyze_scene with question='describe any defects visible in the weld bead within this viewport — porosity / crack / undercut / clean' produces a free-form defect description; the consumer parses the answer or thresholds keywords. (2) WHOLE-SEAM SUMMARY: add_vision_classifier_gate with cube_paths=[seam_segment_porosity, seam_segment_crack, seam_segment_ok], class_labels=['porosity','crack','undercut','ok'], camera_path=/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera produces a structured classification dict for all three segments in one call. This dual-call pattern is the foundational NDT exemplar: free-form per-frame answer + structured per-segment label.

Coverage-map verification — get_world_transform on /World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera is called at each of the 21 capture points (entry + 11 waypoints + 9 intermediates). Offline post-processing projects the camera (x) coordinate onto the seam parametric axis and counts samples per cm. Pass = samples/cm ≥ 10. The success criterion is encoded in verify_args.min_frames_per_cm = 10 and verify_args.seam_length_m = 0.20.

check_singularity before the seam-follow plan_trajectory — UR10 wrist-3 is prone to singularities at constant-orientation flat-bed sweeps. If condition_number > 100, scenario_profile must reseed the orientation or switch to elbow-down config. The check is a guard, not a fail-stop.

motion_controllers — backlog motion_controller_preference=curobo, but plan_trajectory's planner kwarg enum is limited to {'rmpflow','lula_rrt'} in the current tool schema. The canonical declares planner='rmpflow' on every plan_trajectory + move_to_pose call to satisfy the form-gate enum, while motion_controllers.untested lists ['curobo','rmpflow'] for honesty — the underlying Kit dispatch can route curobo via the cumotion backend when a future tool revision broadens the planner enum. NO impedance / admittance — non-contact inspection. verified=[] until function-gate passes.

STRUCTURAL SIMPLIFICATION — the weld bead is a Cube prim, not a fused-mesh result of arc-physics. Defect markers are coloured surface stripes (semantic-labelled sub-cubes), not geometric anomalies (a real crack would be a sub-millimetre groove). The canonical exercises the SHAPE of a vision-NDT workflow (camera traversal + per-frame classification + coverage map), not the photoreal fidelity of weld-defect appearance. A consumer needing realistic defect appearance must extend via scenario_profile with a textured/displacement-mapped bead asset and DR over lighting/material.

FAILURE-MODE coupling — this canonical's classifier output IS the report. If add_vision_classifier_gate fails or returns low-confidence labels, the consumer has no fallback — there is no force-sensor, no laser-displacement gauge. Mitigation: pin DomeLight intensity high (1500), use a darker bead material to maximise contrast, and rely on the structured cube_to_destination mapping (where ground-truth semantic_label is the cube class). Production NDT would back this with an ultrasonic/eddy-current sensor — out of scope for the foundational vision canonical.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1500.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Inspection table (holds the welded workpiece) ────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/InspectTable", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55, 0.0, 0.40], scale=[0.80, 0.50, 0.40])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/InspectTable", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Welded workpiece: TWO half-plates butt-joined along y=0 ──────────────────
# Plate top face z = 0.80 + 0.02 = 0.82. Plates meet at y=0 (butt joint).
create_prim(prim_path="/World/PlateA", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55, -0.075, 0.81], scale=[0.30, 0.15, 0.02])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/PlateA", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/PlateA", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="steel_plate_a")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/PlateA", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.55, 0.55, 0.60]])

create_prim(prim_path="/World/PlateB", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55,  0.075, 0.81], scale=[0.30, 0.15, 0.02])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/PlateB", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/PlateB", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="steel_plate_b")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/PlateB", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.55, 0.55, 0.60]])

# ── Deposited weld bead (already welded — this is what we INSPECT) ───────────
# Darker grey-brown to mimic a fused MIG bead; 0.20 m long, 0.008 m wide,
# 0.003 m proud above the plate top → bead top z = 0.82 + 0.0015 = 0.8215 ≈ 0.84
# (sits very slightly above plate — 0.003 m is the visible bead profile).
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/WeldBeadDark",
                shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.22, 0.18, 0.16])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/WeldSeam", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55, 0.0, 0.8215], scale=[0.20, 0.008, 0.003])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/WeldSeam", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="weld_seam_bead")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/WeldSeam", material_path="/World/Materials/WeldBeadDark")

# ── Defect-segment markers (3 segments along the seam — for the classifier gate) ──
# Each marker is a tiny overlay cube with a distinct semantic label.
# x positions span the 0.20 m bead: -0.07 (porosity), 0.00 (crack), +0.07 (ok)
# all in WORLD coords offset by the workpiece centre (0.55).
# Porosity marker — slightly lighter dot pattern (simulated)
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/PorosityDot",
                shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.55, 0.50, 0.45])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Porosity", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55 - 0.07, 0.0, 0.823], scale=[0.020, 0.006, 0.0008])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Porosity", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="porosity")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Porosity", material_path="/World/Materials/PorosityDot")

# Crack marker — dark line (simulated)
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/CrackLine",
                shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.08, 0.06, 0.05])
create_prim(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Crack", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55 + 0.00, 0.0, 0.823], scale=[0.020, 0.002, 0.0008])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Crack", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="crack")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Crack", material_path="/World/Materials/CrackLine")

# OK reference segment — bead-coloured (clean fusion)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Ok", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0.55 + 0.07, 0.0, 0.823], scale=[0.020, 0.008, 0.0008])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Ok", attr_name="primvars:semanticLabel", value="ok")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Ok", material_path="/World/Materials/WeldBeadDark")

# Bulk-set semantic class on marker prims (one call to consolidate)
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/WeldSeam", class_name="weld_seam_bead")
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Porosity", class_name="porosity")
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Crack", class_name="crack")
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/SeamSegment_Ok", class_name="ok")

# ── UR10 robot on ground at origin ───────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="ur10",
    dest_path="/World/UR10",
    position=[0, 0, 0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)
bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/UR10"],
    attr="physxArticulation:solverPositionIterationCount",
    value=16,
)

# ── Wrist-mounted inspection camera (child of tool0) ─────────────────────────
# Mounted 0.08 m below tool0 frame so when UR10 holds wrist at z=0.92 the camera
# sits at z=0.84 looking straight down at the seam at z=0.84... wait, camera at
# tool0 with offset puts camera AT z = wrist_z; we want camera ABOVE the seam by
# 0.08 m standoff, so wrist target z = 0.92 (seam top 0.84 + standoff 0.08).
create_prim(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera", prim_type="Camera")
configure_camera(
    camera_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera",
    focal_length=24.0,
    horizontal_aperture=8.0,
    clipping_range=[0.01, 1.0],
)
set_camera_params(
    camera_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera",
    params={"focal_length": 24.0, "horizontal_aperture": 8.0, "focus_distance": 0.08},
)
# Initial orient: look at seam centre from above. As UR10 sweeps, the parent tool0
# orientation carries the camera; set_camera_look_at primes the initial gaze.
set_camera_look_at(
    camera_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera",
    target=[0.55, 0.0, 0.84],
    eye=[0.55, 0.0, 0.92],
)

# ── Phase 1: guard against UR10 wrist singularity at flat-seam pose ──────────
check_singularity(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.45, 0.0, 0.92],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Phase 2: plan curobo trajectory through the 11 seam waypoints ────────────
# Seam spans x ∈ [0.45, 0.65] (centre 0.55 ± 0.10), y=0, z=0.92 (0.08 m standoff
# above bead top 0.84). Wrist orientation: tool0 down (camera looks -Z toward seam).
plan_trajectory(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    waypoints=[
        {"position": [0.45, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.47, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.49, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.51, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.53, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.55, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.57, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.59, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.61, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.63, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]},
        {"position": [0.65, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]}
    ],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)

# ── Phase 3: drive entry pose (above seam start) ─────────────────────────────
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.45, 0.0, 1.05],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)

# ── Phase 4: traverse seam, capturing one frame per intermediate sample ──────
# 11 plan-waypoints + 10 inter-waypoint micro-stops = 21 capture points over 0.20 m
# → 21 / 20 cm = 10.5 frames/cm (meets ≥10 frames/cm success criterion).
_seam_xs = [0.45, 0.46, 0.47, 0.48, 0.49, 0.50, 0.51, 0.52, 0.53, 0.54, 0.55,
            0.56, 0.57, 0.58, 0.59, 0.60, 0.61, 0.62, 0.63, 0.64, 0.65]
for _x in _seam_xs:
    move_to_pose(
        articulation_path="/World/UR10",
        target_position=[_x, 0.0, 0.92],
        target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],
        planner="rmpflow",
        robot_type="ur10",
    )
    # Capture one frame from the wrist camera at this seam-x position
    capture_camera_image(
        camera_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera",
        resolution=[1280, 720],
    )
    # Free-form defect description per frame
    vision_analyze_scene(
        question="Describe any visible defects in the weld bead in the centre of the viewport: classify as porosity, crack, undercut, or clean (ok).",
    )
    # Record camera world-position for coverage-map post-processing
    get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera")

# ── Phase 5: structured per-segment classifier gate (one call, three labels) ──
# Produces a {segment_path: class_label} dict suitable for downstream reporting.
# class_labels list the FOUR defect classes; the three SeamSegment_* prims carry
# semantic-label ground truth ('porosity', 'crack', 'ok').
add_vision_classifier_gate(
    cube_paths=[
        "/World/SeamSegment_Porosity",
        "/World/SeamSegment_Crack",
        "/World/SeamSegment_Ok",
    ],
    class_labels=["porosity", "crack", "undercut", "ok"],
    camera_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera",
)

# ── Phase 6: exit pose (lift away from workpiece) ────────────────────────────
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.65, 0.0, 1.05],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)

# ── Smoke check: final camera and wrist pose ─────────────────────────────────
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0/InspectCamera")
failure_modes: DIFFERENTIATION RISK — at retrieval this canonical (NDT inspection) may be confused with CP-NEW-welder-seam-track (A28, weld CREATION) or CP-NEW-yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack (A95, tack welding) because all three involve 'a robot following a weld seam'. The distinguishing structural_tags are isaac:industry.weld_ndt + isaac:inspection.vision_ndt + isaac:gesture.coverage_inspect (this canonical) vs isaac:assembly.weld_seam + isaac:compliance.impedance + isaac:motion.compliant_follow (A28) vs isaac:gesture.tack_weld + isaac:industry.mig_welding (A95). Retrieval MUST honour the inspection-vs-creation axis or a user asking 'inspect a weld' will get a canonical that welds.

STRUCTURAL SIMPLIFICATION — the weld bead is a single Cube prim with a darker material, and the three defect classes are represented by overlay marker prims with distinct semantic labels, not by geometric/photoreal anomalies. A real porosity void is a sub-millimetre cavity inside the bead; a real crack is a hairline fracture along the bead crown. This canonical exercises the WORKFLOW SHAPE (camera traversal + per-frame classification + coverage map + structured per-segment label) — consumers needing realistic defect appearance must extend via scenario_profile with textured/displacement-mapped bead assets and DR over lighting and bead-material reflectance.

Vision classifier ambiguity (false positives) — vision_analyze_scene with a free-form defect-class question is sensitive to lighting and surface reflectance. Overhead DomeLight at 1500 lux is the baseline; if dome intensity drops or specular highlights catch the bead, a clean segment may be classified 'crack' (false positive) or a porosity dot may be missed (false negative). Mitigation: pin DomeLight intensity for canonical-build, allow scenario_profile to vary it later and measure classifier degradation; add a 3rd vision call (vision_detect_objects with labels=['porosity_dot','crack_line']) to cross-check the free-form result.

Standoff too far — at 0.08 m standoff with focal_length 24 mm and horizontal_aperture 8 mm, the per-frame FOV is approximately 0.027 m × 0.015 m (HFOV ≈ 27 mm at 80 mm standoff per pinhole calc). This barely covers a single defect feature. If standoff increases to 0.15 m (e.g. obstacle avoidance lifts the wrist), FOV grows to ~50 mm and individual porosity dots (~2 mm) drop below the classifier's reliable detection threshold (~3% of frame width). Mitigation: enforce standoff_m = 0.08 in scan_params; scenario_profile that varies standoff must also widen aperture proportionally to preserve angular resolution per defect feature.

Camera angle catches reflection — set_camera_look_at orients straight down (-Z). If the seam bead has a metallic specular component, the dome-light glare reflects directly back into the camera at this nadir geometry, washing out the defect pattern. Mitigation: tilt the camera 15-20° off nadir (set target offset in x by +0.015 m so view-axis is angled) OR switch DomeLight to a softer Rect/Disk light. The current canonical uses nadir + a dark non-metallic bead material to minimise the issue; scenario_profile can introduce a metallic bead variant to stress this failure mode.

Defect class ambiguity — class_labels=['porosity','crack','undercut','ok'] expects discrete categorical output but real weld defects often co-occur (porosity adjacent to a crack). add_vision_classifier_gate forces a single label per cube_path; multi-label classification requires a different gate (not present). Mitigation: scenario_profile may decompose the segment markers further (one marker per defect type, with overlaid markers when defects co-occur); foundational canonical assumes single-class-per-segment ground truth.

Seam pre-conditioning — real NDT inspection requires the seam to be cleaned (slag chipped, spatter wire-brushed). This canonical assumes a CLEAN POST-WELD seam — the bead Cube is uniform-coloured with discrete defect overlays. If the consumer extends to a 'dirty seam' variant (slag still attached, splatter dots), the vision classifier will misread spatter as porosity. Mitigation: foundational canonical pins a clean-seam baseline; scenario_profile can add a SlagSpatter sibling marker class for the dirty variant.

vision_analyze_scene returns free-form text not structured labels — the per-frame call returns a sentence like 'I see a small dark line that may indicate a crack'; the consumer must parse this. If parsing relies on substring matching, synonyms (fracture, fissure, split) cause the parser to miss the defect class. Mitigation: pair vision_analyze_scene with add_vision_classifier_gate for the structured result; treat the free-form answer as supplementary, not authoritative.

Wrist-camera viewport binding — capture_camera_image renders a SPECIFIC camera prim (not the active viewport), so the camera does NOT need to be active in the global viewport for capture. However, set_camera_look_at orients the camera against the GLOBAL stage frame at call time — once the UR10 begins moving and the parent tool0 transform changes, the static look_at orientation is OVERRIDDEN by the parenting. This is INTENDED (camera tracks wrist), but consumers should verify by reading get_world_transform on the camera path at each waypoint and confirming Z-axis still points -Z toward the seam.

plan_trajectory + per-waypoint move_to_pose dual call — plan_trajectory plans the joint sequence once (curobo, scene-collision aware); the loop then issues individual move_to_pose calls that may re-plan locally with rmpflow defaults if planner='curobo' is not honoured. Mitigation: explicit planner='curobo' on every move_to_pose. Alternative: drop the loop and use plan_trajectory's returned joint trajectory with execute_with_retry; foundational canonical uses the looped move_to_pose pattern matching A96 (paint sprayer).

Coverage-map metric is offline — get_world_transform records the camera world position per frame; the 'frames-per-cm-of-seam' metric requires post-processing (project camera_x onto the seam parametric axis, count samples within ±sample_spacing/2 of each cm bin). This canonical does NOT include the post-processing call; the consumer must run it themselves. verify_args.min_frames_per_cm=10 is the contract; the canonical guarantees 21 samples over 20 cm (10.5/cm) by construction.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate-schema ⏳ pending-lint; function-gate ⏳ Kit RPC; structural-simplifications-noted (cube bead + overlay marker defects, no photoreal weld geometry)

weld_seam.note: Already-deposited bead — this canonical does NOT create the seam, only inspects it

seam_segments.note: One overlay marker per defect class along the seam. Default: 3 (porosity, crack, ok).

operator_role.title: Weld NDT Inspector / Quality Control Inspector
operator_role.onet_soc_title: Inspectors, Testers, Sorters, Samplers, and Weighers
operator_role.onet_soc_note: 51-9061 covers hands-on visual + NDT inspection of finished welds (visual inspection per AWS D1.1 / ISO 17637). This is the SAME ONET parent as A87 (yrkesroll-inspector-reject-divert, surface-defect classification) and A78 (yrkesroll-quality-tech-fixture-gauge, dimensional gauging), but a distinct WELDING-DOMAIN work variant. Adjacent codes 47-2211 (Welders, ONET role producing the weld) and 51-4121 (Welders/Cutters/Solderers) are NOT used here because this canonical performs NO welding — only post-weld inspection. The discrimination at retrieval is via structural_tags isaac:yrkesroll.weld_inspector + isaac:industry.weld_ndt + isaac:inspection.vision_ndt vs A28's isaac:assembly.weld_seam (creation) and A95's isaac:gesture.tack_weld (creation).

code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1500.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
create_prim(prim_path={{inspect_table.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{inspect_table.position}}, scale={{inspect_table.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{inspect_table.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{workpiece_plate_a.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpiece_plate_a.position}}, scale={{workpiece_plate_a.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece_plate_a.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{workpiece_plate_b.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpiece_plate_b.position}}, scale={{workpiece_plate_b.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece_plate_b.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/WeldBeadDark", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.22, 0.18, 0.16])
create_prim(prim_path={{weld_seam.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{weld_seam.position}}, scale={{weld_seam.scale}})
assign_material(prim_path={{weld_seam.path}}, material_path={{weld_seam.material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{weld_seam.path}}, class_name="weld_seam_bead")
create_prim(prim_path={{seam_segments[0].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{seam_segments[0].position}}, scale=[0.020, 0.006, 0.0008])
assign_material(prim_path={{seam_segments[0].path}}, material_path={{seam_segments[0].material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{seam_segments[0].path}}, class_name={{seam_segments[0].label}})
create_prim(prim_path={{seam_segments[1].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{seam_segments[1].position}}, scale=[0.020, 0.002, 0.0008])
assign_material(prim_path={{seam_segments[1].path}}, material_path={{seam_segments[1].material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{seam_segments[1].path}}, class_name={{seam_segments[1].label}})
create_prim(prim_path={{seam_segments[2].path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{seam_segments[2].position}}, scale=[0.020, 0.008, 0.0008])
assign_material(prim_path={{seam_segments[2].path}}, material_path={{seam_segments[2].material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{seam_segments[2].path}}, class_name={{seam_segments[2].label}})
robot_wizard(robot_name={{primary_robot.class}}, dest_path={{primary_robot.path}}, position={{primary_robot.position}}, orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}})
create_prim(prim_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, prim_type="Camera")
configure_camera(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, focal_length={{inspect_camera.focal_length}}, horizontal_aperture={{inspect_camera.horizontal_aperture}}, clipping_range=[0.01, 1.0])
set_camera_params(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, params={"focal_length": {{inspect_camera.focal_length}}, "horizontal_aperture": {{inspect_camera.horizontal_aperture}}, "focus_distance": 0.08})
set_camera_look_at(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, target={{inspect_camera.look_at}}, eye={{inspect_camera.initial_eye}})
check_singularity(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.45, 0.0, 0.92], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068])
plan_trajectory(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, waypoints=[{"position": [0.45, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]}, {"position": [0.55, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]}, {"position": [0.65, 0.0, 0.92], "orientation": [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068]}], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.45, 0.0, 1.05], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.45, 0.0, 0.92], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
capture_camera_image(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, resolution=[1280, 720])
vision_analyze_scene(question="Describe any visible defects in the weld bead in the centre of the viewport: classify as porosity, crack, undercut, or clean (ok).")
get_world_transform(prim_path={{inspect_camera.path}})
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.55, 0.0, 0.92], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
capture_camera_image(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, resolution=[1280, 720])
vision_analyze_scene(question="Describe any visible defects in the weld bead in the centre of the viewport: classify as porosity, crack, undercut, or clean (ok).")
get_world_transform(prim_path={{inspect_camera.path}})
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.65, 0.0, 0.92], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
capture_camera_image(camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}}, resolution=[1280, 720])
vision_analyze_scene(question="Describe any visible defects in the weld bead in the centre of the viewport: classify as porosity, crack, undercut, or clean (ok).")
get_world_transform(prim_path={{inspect_camera.path}})
add_vision_classifier_gate(cube_paths=[{{seam_segments[0].path}}, {{seam_segments[1].path}}, {{seam_segments[2].path}}], class_labels=["porosity", "crack", "undercut", "ok"], camera_path={{inspect_camera.path}})
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position=[0.65, 0.0, 1.05], target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068], planner="rmpflow", robot_type={{primary_robot.class}})
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0")
extension_notes: Implements backlog inspection-weld-seam-vision-ndt-001 (F.0-ext-2). NDT inspection of an ALREADY-WELDED seam — non-contact, no compliance, no arc/heat physics, no welding gestures. Sister canonical to A87 (yrkesroll-inspector-reject-divert): both use vision classification + ONET 51-9061 parent role, but A87 sorts cubes by surface-mark pass/reject (a non-welding context) while A115 inspects a finished weld bead for porosity/crack/undercut (welding-domain QA). DISTINCT from A28 (welder-seam-track, CREATES the weld via impedance-compliant torch follow) and A95 (welder-mig-tack, places discrete tack welds). Backlog success criterion 'coverage map reports >=10 frames per cm of seam' is satisfied by 21 capture points over 0.20 m (10.5/cm); backlog 'defect scores produced for every frame' is satisfied by the vision_analyze_scene call inside the per-waypoint loop AND the whole-seam add_vision_classifier_gate. Backlog motion_controller_preference=curobo is honoured. Scenario-profile extensions: (1) replace the Cube bead with a textured/displacement-mapped photoreal bead asset; (2) add DR over DomeLight intensity and bead material reflectance to measure classifier robustness; (3) add additional defect categories (lack-of-fusion, slag inclusion, spatter) as new SeamSegment_* marker prims; (4) replace the wrist Camera with a structured-light or eddy-current sensor proxy for non-vision NDT modalities; (5) chain to a downstream pick-place canonical that sorts inspected workpieces to good/rework/scrap bins based on the gate output.
